name: Popa
description_short: POPA is a federal labor union representing U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) patent examiners, providing legal defense, grievance representation, and workplace advocacy.
description_long: POPA (Patent Office Professional Association) is a federal labor union established to serve patent professionals working at the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office. The organization functions as an employee advocacy and legal defense organization rather than a consumer finance company. POPA provides comprehensive representation and support to its members through collective bargaining agreements, legal defense services, and workplace grievance management. The organization has negotiated a Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) covering the period 2024-2029 that governs working conditions, compensation, and benefits for patent examiners. POPA is not a financial services provider or lender of any kind. The website content exclusively addresses employment matters, labor union functions, and federal workplace policies relevant to USPTO patent examiners. There is no mention of loans, credit products, financial assistance programs, or any payday alternative services. The miscategorization as a payday alternative appears to be a database error, as POPA has zero connection to consumer finance. The organization operates as a free-help resource specifically for its federal employee members, providing legal advocacy, grievance filing assistance, and representation in workplace disputes. POPA's 24-hour hotline and legal defense fund exist solely to support USPTO employees facing proposed removals, suspensions, or investigatory meetings. The union emphasizes workplace rights, fair treatment, and collective bargaining rather than financial services.
services: Legal defense representation for proposed removals and suspensions
Grievance filing and association grievance management
Collective bargaining agreement representation and negotiation
Workplace facility and transfer dispute resolution
Federal workers legal defense fund access
pros: 24-hour emergency hotline (Hotline@popa.org) for members facing proposed removals or investigatory meetings
Comprehensive legal defense fund to support members in workplace disputes
Negotiated Collective Bargaining Agreement (2024-2029) protecting member compensation and benefits
Free grievance representation and assistance with unfair labor practice claims
Extensive workplace resources covering leave policies, telework, performance management, and workplace accommodations
Joint Labor Management Committee (JLM) participation ensuring member voice in workplace decisions
Legal representation support for members in proposed suspensions and removal proceedings
cons: Exclusively serves USPTO patent examiners only—not open to general public or other federal employees
Membership-based model; benefits only available to dues-paying members
Website navigation is complex and document-heavy, making it difficult for non-members to understand services
best_for: U.S. Patent and Trademark Office patent examiners seeking legal defense and workplace representation
Federal employees dealing with proposed disciplinary actions or investigatory meetings
USPTO staff needing grievance filing assistance and labor union advocacy
diagnosis: POPA is a federal labor union exclusively serving USPTO patent examiners and is fundamentally misclassified as a payday alternative lender. This organization provides zero consumer finance services and should be recategorized as a free-help/advocacy organization. It is only relevant to federal patent examiners employed by the USPTO.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
